question_title: Will OpenSSL publish a HIGH or CRITICAL security advisory before May 1, 2026?
summary: The key HIGH advisory (CVE-2025-15467) was published January 27, 2026 — confirmed by 5+ independent sources — which falls before the February 24 start date. The question therefore requires a genuinely NEW HIGH/CRITICAL advisory between now and May 1, 2026. With a historical Poisson rate of ~1 HIGH/CRITICAL advisory per year, the 49-day remaining window gives ~12.6% probability. Adding a small (5%) probability that the vulnerabilities.html page was first updated after Feb 24 (based on the timeline listing the advisory under March 10, 2026 alongside patch releases), the combined probability is ~17%.

factors.description: CVE-2025-15467 (HIGH) was published January 27, 2026 — before the Feb 24 start date. Confirmed by Datadog ('Today, on January 27th...'), Orca Security, AWS ('Public on 2026-01-27'), oss-security mailing list, and Red Hat (fix published Jan 28). Does not count for resolution.
OpenSSL timeline lists advisory under March 10, 2026 alongside patch releases. Small possibility vulnerabilities.html was first updated after Feb 24, which would make CVE-2025-15467 HIGH count.
Historical base rate: ~1 HIGH/CRITICAL OpenSSL advisory per year (2020-2026 data from timeline). Poisson probability for 49 remaining days = 12.6%.
AI-powered discovery (AISLE found all 12 January CVEs) may modestly increase future vulnerability discovery rate above historical baseline.
Recent large patch batch (12 CVEs, Jan 2026) may temporarily reduce near-term HIGH findings as most accessible issues were addressed.
No evidence of pending HIGH/CRITICAL advisory — no news, pre-notifications, or upcoming disclosures found in searches.
Status quo persistence: 49 days is short for a rare event. Most similar windows pass without a HIGH/CRITICAL advisory.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 17% probability (logit: -1.6)**

Key reasoning:

1. **The existing HIGH advisory doesn't count.** CVE-2025-15467 (HIGH, CVSS 9.8) was published January 27, 2026 — confirmed by Datadog, Orca Security, AWS, the oss-security mailing list, and Red Hat's next-day fix. This falls before the February 24 start date.

2. **Base rate is modest.** OpenSSL publishes ~1 HIGH/CRITICAL advisory per year historically (2020-2026). For the remaining 49-day window, a Poisson model gives ~12.6% probability of a new one.

3. **Small interpretation upside.** The OpenSSL timeline lists the advisory under "10 Mar 2026" alongside patch releases. There's a ~5% chance the vulnerabilities.html page was first updated after Feb 24, which would make the existing HIGH advisory count — contributing ~4.8pp to the total.

4. **No pending disclosures found.** No news or pre-notifications suggest an imminent HIGH/CRITICAL advisory.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will OpenSSL publish a HIGH or CRITICAL security advisory before May 1, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42511)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if OpenSSL publishes at least one new advisory on or after February 24, 2026 and before May 1, 2026 that is rated HIGH or CRITICAL. This question resolves to no otherwise. The primary source is OpenSSL’s vulnerabilities/advisories page at https://www.openssl.org/news/vulnerabilities.html
fine_print: Use OpenSSL’s severity rating as shown on the advisory listing. Count advisories newly posted in the period (not older advisories merely edited). If disputes arise, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions. Candidate Sources: OpenSSL News page (backup) https://www.openssl.org/news/newslog.html | OpenSSL GitHub releases (backup) https://github.com/openssl/openssl/releases
background_info:
This forecasts whether a high-severity OpenSSL issue will be disclosed soon, relevant for broad infrastructure patch planning.
